74	// isUpdateTargetDirty returns true when filePath exists, is tracked by git,
75	// and has uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged). Untracked files return false.
76	// Returns false on any error (git unavailable, not a repo, etc.).
77	func isUpdateTargetDirty(workingDir, filePath string) bool {
78		abs := filePath
79		if !filepath.IsAbs(abs) {
80			abs = filepath.Join(workingDir, filePath)
81		}
82		if _, err := os.Stat(abs); os.IsNotExist(err) {
83			return false
84		}
85	
86		rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, abs)
87		if err != nil {
88			rel = filePath
89		}
90	
91		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
92		defer cancel()
93		cmd := gitpkg.Command(ctx, workingDir, "status", "--porcelain", "--", rel)
94		out, err := cmd.Output()
95		if err != nil {
96			return false
97		}
98		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
99			if len(line) < 2 {
100				continue
101			}
102			// XY: X=index status, Y=worktree status. "??" = untracked — skip.
103			if line[:2] != "??" {
104				return true
105			}
106		}
107		return false
108	}

166	// backupUpdateFile copies filePath to backupBase/<path-relative-to-workingDir>.
167	func backupUpdateFile(workingDir, filePath, backupBase string) error {
168		abs := filePath
169		if !filepath.IsAbs(abs) {
170			abs = filepath.Join(workingDir, filePath)
171		}
172		rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, abs)
173		if err != nil {
174			rel = filepath.Base(abs)
175		}
176		dst := filepath.Join(backupBase, rel)
177		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o755); err != nil {
178			return err
179		}
180		data, err := os.ReadFile(abs)
181		if err != nil {
182			return err
183		}
184		return os.WriteFile(dst, data, 0o644)
185	}

187	// performUpdate checks GitHub for the latest version of each installed plugin
188	// and updates any that are outdated (or all if --force). Always refreshes the
189	// embedded `ddx` skill and the AGENTS.md block so projects that ran `ddx init`
190	// under an older DDx version pick up current skill content without re-running
191	// init. Binary updates are intentionally explicit via `ddx upgrade`; `ddx
192	// update` must not replace a locally-built dogfood binary with the latest
193	// public release.
194	func performUpdate(workingDir string, opts *UpdateOptions) (*UpdateResult, error) {
195		// Pre-check: detect dirty update targets before writing anything (atomic
196		// refuse — no file is mutated if any target is dirty without --discard-local).
197		dirtyFiles, _ := collectDirtyUpdateTargets(workingDir)
198		if len(dirtyFiles) > 0 && !opts.DiscardLocal {
199			var sb strings.Builder
200			sb.WriteString("ddx update: uncommitted changes in files that would be overwritten:\n")
201			for _, f := range dirtyFiles {
202				sb.WriteString("  ")
203				if rel, err := filepath.Rel(workingDir, f); err == nil {
204					sb.WriteString(rel)
205				} else {
206					sb.WriteString(f)
207				}
208				sb.WriteString("\n")
209			}
210			sb.WriteString("\nCommit or stash these changes first, or use --force --discard-local to overwrite anyway.")
211			return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s", sb.String())
212		}
213	
214		// Backup dirty files before overwriting when --discard-local is set.
215		var backupPath string
216		if len(dirtyFiles) > 0 && opts.DiscardLocal {
217			backupPath = ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, "update-backup",
218				time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405"))
219			for _, f := range dirtyFiles {
220				_ = backupUpdateFile(workingDir, f, backupPath)
221			}
222		}
223	
224		// Refresh the shipped `ddx` skill copy + AGENTS.md block first, regardless
225		// of whether any plugins are installed. This is what lets older projects
226		// pick up new SKILL.md / reference/*.md content without re-init.
227		refreshShippedSkills(workingDir)
228	
229		state, err := registry.LoadState()
230		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
231			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. No packages installed.", BackupPath: backupPath}, nil
232		}
233	
234		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
235	
236		var updated []string
237	
238		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
239			// Filter to specific target if requested.
240			if opts.Resource != "" && entry.Name != opts.Resource {
241				continue
242			}
243	
244			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
245			if err != nil {
246				continue // not in registry, skip
247			}
248	
249			// Fetch actual latest version from GitHub.
250			latestVersion := pkg.Version
251			if release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source); err == nil {
252				latestVersion = strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
253			}
254	
255			if !opts.Force && entry.Version == latestVersion {
256				continue
257			}
258	
259			// Install the latest version.
260			installPkg := *pkg
261			installPkg.Version = latestVersion
262			newEntry, err := registry.InstallPackage(&installPkg, workingDir)
263			if err != nil {
264				return nil, fmt.Errorf("updating %s: %w", entry.Name, err)
265			}
266			state.AddOrUpdate(newEntry)
267			updated = append(updated, entry.Name+" "+entry.Version+" → "+latestVersion)
268		}
269	
270		if err := registry.SaveState(state); err != nil {
271			return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving state: %w", err)
272		}
273	
274		if len(updated) == 0 {
275			return &UpdateResult{Success: true, Message: "Shipped skills refreshed. All packages are up to date.", BackupPath: backupPath}, nil
276		}
277	
278		return &UpdateResult{
279			Success:      true,
280			Message:      "Updated: " + strings.Join(updated, ", "),
281			UpdatedFiles: updated,
282			BackupPath:   backupPath,
283		}, nil
284	}
285	
286	// refreshShippedSkills re-copies the embedded `ddx` skill into the project's
287	// skill directories and refreshes the AGENTS.md DDx block. Safe to call on
288	// every `ddx update` because skills.Install with Force=true handles the
289	// "existing files should be updated" case, and generateAgentsMD's
290	// marker-based merge is idempotent. Stale pre-consolidation skill dirs
291	// (ddx-bead, ddx-run, etc.) are swept by cleanupBootstrapSkills.
292	func refreshShippedSkills(workingDir string) {
293		bootstrapSkillNames := []string{"ddx"}
294		for _, dir := range []string{
295			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".agents", "skills"),
296			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".claude", "skills"),
297		} {
298			_ = os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755)
299			cleanupBootstrapSkills(dir, bootstrapSkillNames)
300		}
301		if err := skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, workingDir, skills.Options{Force: true}); err != nil {
302			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: skill install failed: %v\n", err)
303		}
304		generateAgentsMD(workingDir)
305	}

345	// Output formatting function
346	func displayUpdateResult(cmd *cobra.Command, result *UpdateResult, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
347		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
348		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
349	
350		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
351		writer := out.(io.Writer)
352	
353		// Handle error cases
354		if !result.Success {
355			if len(result.Conflicts) > 0 {
356				return handleConflictOutput(out, result.Conflicts, opts)
357			}
358			_, _ = red.Fprintln(writer, "❌", result.Message)
359			return nil
360		}
361	
362		// Handle check mode
363		if opts.Check {
364			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Checking for updates...")
365			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Fetching latest changes from master repository...")
366			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Available updates:")
367			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Changes since last update:")
368			return nil
369		}
370	
371		// Handle dry-run mode
372		if opts.DryRun {
373			return displayDryRunResult(out, result, opts)
374		}
375	
376		// Display success message
377		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "✅", result.Message)
378		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
379	
380		// Show updated files
381		if len(result.UpdatedFiles) > 0 {
382			_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "📦 Updated resources:")
383			for _, file := range result.UpdatedFiles {
384				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "  • %s\n", file)
385			}
386			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
387		}
388	
389		// Show backup info
390		if result.BackupPath != "" {
391			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "💾 Backup created at: %s\n", result.BackupPath)
392			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
393		}
394	
395		return nil
396	}

398	func handleConflictOutput(out interface{}, conflicts []ConflictInfo, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
399		writer := out.(io.Writer)
400		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
401		cyan := color.New(color.FgCyan)
402		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)

408		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
409	
410		// Display detailed conflict information
411		for i, conflict := range conflicts {
412			_, _ = red.Fprintf(writer, "❌ Conflict %d: %s (line %d)\n", i+1, conflict.FilePath, conflict.LineNumber)
413			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
414		}

416		// Provide resolution guidance

426		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --theirs           Same as --strategy=theirs")
427		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")

433		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")

437		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "💡 Examples:")

441		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --abort             # Cancel and restore")

147	func newPackageArchiveServer(t *testing.T, payload []byte) *httptest.Server {
148		t.Helper()
149	
150		return httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
151			if !strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/archive/refs/tags/v1.0.0.tar.gz") {
152				t.Errorf("unexpected archive path: %s", r.URL.Path)
153			}

155			_, _ = w.Write(payload)

277	// and embedded-FS entrypoint share the same skill mapping behavior for a
278	// fixture package — same recorded files, same on-disk layout.
279	func TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore(t *testing.T) {

293		remoteFiles := append([]string(nil), remoteEntry.Files...)
294		fsFiles := append([]string(nil), fsEntry.Files...)
295		sort.Strings(remoteFiles)
296		sort.Strings(fsFiles)

298	
299		// And the same skill files actually exist under each project root.

312	// canonical library/package.yaml declares package-local skill sources
313	// (i.e. source: skills/) so the embedded default-package install copies
314	// the DDx skill out of library/skills/ rather than out of a checked-in
315	// .agents/skills mirror.
316	func TestDefaultDDxPackageManifestUsesPackageLocalSkillSource(t *testing.T) {
317		manifestPath := filepath.Join("..", "..", "..", "library", "package.yaml")
318		pkg, issues, err := LoadPackageManifest(filepath.Dir(manifestPath))
319		require.NoError(t, err, "library/package.yaml must load cleanly")

324		for _, m := range pkg.Install.Skills {

331	// default-package tree used by the binary actually carries the canonical
332	// library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md so offline `ddx init` installs a real skill.
333	func TestDefaultPackageEmbedCopyIncludesDDxSkill(t *testing.T) {
334		data, err := iofs.ReadFile(defaultplugin.FS(), "skills/ddx/SKILL.md")
335		require.NoError(t, err, "embedded default package must include skills/ddx/SKILL.md")
336		require.NotEmpty(t, data, "embedded skills/ddx/SKILL.md must not be empty")
337	
338		canonical, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join("..", "..", "..", "library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"))
339		require.NoError(t, err, "canonical library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md must exist on disk")

341			"embedded skills/ddx/SKILL.md must match the canonical library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md (run `make copy-skills` to sync)")

344	// offlineTransport fails any HTTP attempt so tests can prove an install path
345	// did not reach the network.
346	type offlineTransport struct{ t *testing.T }
347	
348	func (o *offlineTransport) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
349		o.t.Helper()

11		"testing"

15		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"

20	// TestMain clears all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
21	// that tests invoking git commands operate on their own temp-dir repositories
22	// rather than any repository inherited from a hook invocation (e.g. GIT_DIR
23	// set by lefthook during pre-commit). Prefix scrub is important — lefthook
24	// sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS etc. that
25	// also leak into test subprocesses and corrupt the shared repo config.
26	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
27		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
30					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
31				}
32			}
33		}
34		// Keep cmd tests hermetic. Background update checks can outlive a single
35		// test and write into another test's temp HOME/TMPDIR, which makes temp-dir
36		// cleanup flaky under the full suite.
37		_ = os.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
38		cleanupTemp := isolateCmdTestTempRoot()
39		code := m.Run()
40		cleanupTemp()
41		os.Exit(code)
42	}
43	
44	func isolateCmdTestTempRoot() func() {
45		tempRoot, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-cmd-tests-*")
46		if err != nil {
47			return func() {}
48		}
49		oldTmpDir, hadTmpDir := os.LookupEnv("TMPDIR")
50		oldTmp, hadTmp := os.LookupEnv("TMP")
51		oldXDG, hadXDG := os.LookupEnv("XDG_DATA_HOME")
52		oldHome, hadHome := os.LookupEnv("HOME")
53		homeDir := filepath.Join(tempRoot, "home")
54		_ = os.MkdirAll(homeDir, 0o755)
55		_ = os.Setenv("TMPDIR", tempRoot)
56		_ = os.Setenv("TMP", tempRoot)
57		_ = os.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", filepath.Join(tempRoot, "xdg"))
58		_ = os.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
59		return func() {
60			if hadTmpDir {
61				_ = os.Setenv("TMPDIR", oldTmpDir)
62			} else {
63				_ = os.Unsetenv("TMPDIR")
64			}
65			if hadTmp {
66				_ = os.Setenv("TMP", oldTmp)
67			} else {
68				_ = os.Unsetenv("TMP")
69			}
70			if hadXDG {
71				_ = os.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", oldXDG)
72			} else {
73				_ = os.Unsetenv("XDG_DATA_HOME")
74			}
75			if hadHome {
76				_ = os.Setenv("HOME", oldHome)
77			} else {
78				_ = os.Unsetenv("HOME")
79			}
80			_ = os.RemoveAll(tempRoot)
81		}
82	}

84	// buildTestLibraryFixture creates a per-test bare git repository that mimics
85	// the upstream ddx library. The repo lives under t.TempDir() and is cleaned up
86	// automatically with the test.
87	func buildTestLibraryFixture(t *testing.T) string {
88		t.Helper()
89	
90		_, filename, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
91		cmdDir := filepath.Dir(filename)
92		fixtureDir := filepath.Join(cmdDir, "..", "test", "fixtures", "ddx-library")
93		fixtureDir, err := filepath.Abs(fixtureDir)
94		require.NoError(t, err)
95	
96		root := t.TempDir()
97		workingRepo := filepath.Join(root, ".test-library")
98		bareRepo := filepath.Join(root, ".test-library.git")
99	
100		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workingRepo, 0o755))
101		require.NoError(t, syncDirectory(fixtureDir, workingRepo))
102	
103		cmds := []struct {
104			args []string
105			dir  string
106		}{
107			{[]string{"git", "init", "-b", "master"}, workingRepo},
108			{[]string{"git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"}, workingRepo},
109			{[]string{"git", "config", "user.name", "Test User"}, workingRepo},
110			{[]string{"git", "add", "."}, workingRepo},
111			{[]string{"git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "Test fixture"}, workingRepo},
112			{[]string{"git", "init", "--bare", bareRepo}, ""},
113			{[]string{"git", "remote", "add", "origin", bareRepo}, workingRepo},
114			{[]string{"git", "push", "origin", "master"}, workingRepo},
115		}
116	
117		for _, c := range cmds {
118			cmd := exec.Command(c.args[0], c.args[1:]...)
119			if c.dir != "" {
120				cmd.Dir = c.dir
121			}
122			cmd.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
123			output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
124			require.NoErrorf(t, err, "failed to run %v\nOutput: %s", c.args, output)
125		}
126	
127		return bareRepo
128	}

130	// syncDirectory copies files from src to dst, preserving directory structure
131	func syncDirectory(src, dst string) error {
132		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
133			if err != nil {
134				return err
135			}
136	
137			// Skip .git directories from source
138			if info.IsDir() && info.Name() == ".git" {
139				return filepath.SkipDir
140			}
141	
142			// Calculate relative path
143			relPath, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
144			if err != nil {
145				return err
146			}
147	
148			dstPath := filepath.Join(dst, relPath)
149	
150			if info.IsDir() {
151				return os.MkdirAll(dstPath, info.Mode())
152			}
153	
154			// Copy file
155			srcFile, err := os.Open(path)
156			if err != nil {
157				return err
158			}
159			defer func() { _ = srcFile.Close() }()
160	
161			dstFile, err := os.Create(dstPath)
162			if err != nil {
163				return err
164			}
165			defer func() { _ = dstFile.Close() }()
166	
167			if _, err := dstFile.ReadFrom(srcFile); err != nil {
168				return err
169			}
170	
171			return os.Chmod(dstPath, info.Mode())
172		})
173	}

175	// TestEnvironment provides isolated testing environment for .ddx/config.yaml
176	type TestEnvironment struct {
177		Dir            string
178		ConfigPath     string
179		LibraryPath    string
180		TestLibraryURL string
181		GitInitialized bool
182	
183		// Optional customizations
184		Platform     string
185		Architecture string
186		HomeDir      string
187	
188		t *testing.T
189	}
190	
191	// TestEnvOption is a functional option for configuring TestEnvironment
192	type TestEnvOption func(*TestEnvironment)
193	
194	// WithGitInit controls whether to initialize a git repository
195	func WithGitInit(init bool) TestEnvOption {
196		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
197			te.GitInitialized = init
198		}
199	}
200	
201	// WithPlatform sets the platform and architecture for installation tests
202	func WithPlatform(platform, arch string) TestEnvOption {
203		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
204			te.Platform = platform
205			te.Architecture = arch
206		}
207	}
208	
209	// WithHomeDir sets a custom home directory
210	func WithHomeDir(homeDir string) TestEnvOption {
211		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
212			te.HomeDir = homeDir
213		}
214	}
215	
216	// WithCustomLibraryURL sets a custom library repository URL
217	func WithCustomLibraryURL(url string) TestEnvOption {
218		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
219			te.TestLibraryURL = url
220		}
221	}
222	
223	// NewTestEnvironment creates a clean test environment with temp directory
224	// By default: creates git repo, uses test-library fixture via file:// URL
225	func NewTestEnvironment(t *testing.T, opts ...TestEnvOption) *TestEnvironment {
226		t.Helper()
227	
228		// Command fixtures should be hermetic. The update checker runs in a
229		// background goroutine and can outlive a test's temp HOME/config dirs.
230		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
231	
232		tempDir := t.TempDir()
233		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ddxroot.DirName)
234		configPath := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml")
235	
236		te := &TestEnvironment{
237			Dir:            tempDir,
238			ConfigPath:     configPath,
239			LibraryPath:    filepath.Join(ddxDir, "library"),
240			GitInitialized: true, // default: init git
241			t:              t,
242		}
243	
244		// Apply options
245		for _, opt := range opts {
246			opt(te)
247		}
248	
249		// Set default test library URL if not customized
250		if te.TestLibraryURL == "" {
251			te.TestLibraryURL = "file://" + buildTestLibraryFixture(t)
252		}
253	
254		// Create .ddx directory
255		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
256	
257		// Initialize git repository if requested
258		if te.GitInitialized {
259			te.initGit()
260		}
261	
262		return te
263	}
264	
265	// initGit initializes a git repository in the test directory
266	func (te *TestEnvironment) initGit() {
267		te.t.Helper()
268	
269		// Strip inherited git env vars so git commands operate on the test
270		// directory's own repository, not any repository inherited from a
271		// hook or parent process (e.g. GIT_DIR set by lefthook).
272		cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
273	
274		// git init
275		gitInit := exec.Command("git", "init")
276		gitInit.Dir = te.Dir
277		gitInit.Env = cleanEnv
278		require.NoError(te.t, gitInit.Run(), "git init should succeed")
279	
280		// git config user.email
281		gitEmail := exec.Command("git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
282		gitEmail.Dir = te.Dir
283		gitEmail.Env = cleanEnv
284		require.NoError(te.t, gitEmail.Run(), "git config user.email should succeed")
285	
286		// git config user.name
287		gitName := exec.Command("git", "config", "user.name", "Test User")
288		gitName.Dir = te.Dir
289		gitName.Env = cleanEnv
290		require.NoError(te.t, gitName.Run(), "git config user.name should succeed")
291	}
292	
293	// CreateConfig creates a config file with the given content
294	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateConfig(content string) {
295		te.t.Helper()
296		require.NoError(te.t, os.WriteFile(te.ConfigPath, []byte(content), 0644))
297	}
298	
299	// CreateDefaultConfig creates a minimal valid config file using test library
300	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateDefaultConfig() {
301		te.t.Helper()
302		content := fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
303	library:
304	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
305	  repository:
306	    url: %s
307	    branch: master
308	persona_bindings: {}
309	`, te.TestLibraryURL)
310		te.CreateConfig(content)
311	}
312	
313	// CreateConfigWithCustomURL creates a config with a custom repository URL
314	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateConfigWithCustomURL(url string) {
315		te.t.Helper()
316		content := fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
317	library:
318	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
319	  repository:
320	    url: %s
321	    branch: master
322	persona_bindings: {}
323	`, url)
324		te.CreateConfig(content)
325	}
326	
327	// RunCommand runs a DDx command in the test environment and returns output
328	func (te *TestEnvironment) RunCommand(args ...string) (string, error) {
329		te.t.Helper()
330		factory := NewCommandFactory(te.Dir)
331		cmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
332		cmd.SetArgs(args)
333	
334		var buf bytes.Buffer
335		cmd.SetOut(&buf)
336		cmd.SetErr(&buf)
337	
338		err := cmd.Execute()
339		return buf.String(), err
340	}
341	
342	// InitWithDDx properly initializes DDx in the test environment using ddx init
343	// If git is initialized, creates an initial commit before running ddx init.
344	// Otherwise uses --no-git flag. In CI environments, always uses --no-git.
345	func (te *TestEnvironment) InitWithDDx(flags ...string) {
346		te.t.Helper()
347	
348		// Check if we should skip git operations
349		hasNoGitFlag := false
350		for _, flag := range flags {
351			if flag == "--no-git" {
352				hasNoGitFlag = true
353				break
354			}
355		}
356	
357		// If git is initialized and we're not using --no-git, create initial commit
358		if te.GitInitialized && !hasNoGitFlag {
359			cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
360			te.CreateFile("README.md", "# Test Project")
361			gitAdd := exec.Command("git", "add", ".")
362			gitAdd.Dir = te.Dir
363			gitAdd.Env = cleanEnv
364			require.NoError(te.t, gitAdd.Run())
365			gitCommit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "-m", "Initial commit")
366			gitCommit.Dir = te.Dir
367			gitCommit.Env = cleanEnv
368			require.NoError(te.t, gitCommit.Run())
369		}
370	
371		// Default flags if none provided
372		if len(flags) == 0 {
373			if te.GitInitialized {
374				// Use --repository and --branch flags to specify test library
375				flags = []string{"--repository", te.TestLibraryURL, "--branch", "master", "--silent", "--skip-claude-injection"}
376			} else {
377				// No git, use --no-git flag
378				flags = []string{"--no-git", "--silent", "--skip-claude-injection"}
379			}
380		}
381	
382		args := append([]string{"init"}, flags...)
383		output, err := te.RunCommand(args...)
384		require.NoError(te.t, err, "init should succeed: %s", output)
385	}
386	
387	// LoadConfig loads the config using ConfigLoader
388	func (te *TestEnvironment) LoadConfig() (*config.Config, error) {
389		loader, err := config.NewConfigLoaderWithWorkingDir(te.Dir)
390		if err != nil {
391			return nil, err
392		}
393		return loader.LoadConfig()
394	}
395	
396	// CreateFile creates any file in the test environment
397	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateFile(relativePath, content string) {
398		te.t.Helper()
399		fullPath := filepath.Join(te.Dir, relativePath)
400		dir := filepath.Dir(fullPath)
401		require.NoError(te.t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755))
402		require.NoError(te.t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0644))
403	}
404	
405	func TestNoSharedTmpFixtureLeaks(t *testing.T) {
406		for _, path := range []string{"/tmp/.test-library", "/tmp/.test-library.git"} {
407			_, err := os.Stat(path)
408			if err == nil {
409				t.Fatalf("unexpected shared fixture path exists: %s", path)
410			}
411			if !os.IsNotExist(err) {
412				t.Fatalf("expected %s to be absent, got: %v", path, err)
413			}
414		}
415	}
416	
417	// NewTestRootCommand creates a fresh root command for tests using isolated temp directory
418	// This is the preferred way to create test commands - it ensures test isolation.
419	func NewTestRootCommand(t *testing.T) *CommandFactory {
420		t.Helper()
421		tempDir := t.TempDir()
422		return NewCommandFactory(tempDir)
423	}
424	
425	// NewTestRootCommandWithDir creates a test command with a specific working directory
426	// Use this when your test needs to operate in a specific directory.
427	func NewTestRootCommandWithDir(dir string) *CommandFactory {
428		return NewCommandFactory(dir)
429	}
430	
431	// executeCommand is a helper to execute commands with captured output (legacy compatibility)
432	// New tests should use TestEnvironment.RunCommand() instead
433	func executeCommand(root *cobra.Command, args ...string) (output string, err error) {
434		buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
435		root.SetOut(buf)
436		root.SetErr(buf)
437		root.SetArgs(args)
438	
439		err = root.Execute()
440		return buf.String(), err
441	}

563	func mustBuildInstallTarball(t *testing.T, rootName string, manifest string) []byte {
564		t.Helper()
565	
566		var buf bytes.Buffer
567		gz := gzip.NewWriter(&buf)
568		tw := tar.NewWriter(gz)
569	
570		writeDir := func(name string) {
571			t.Helper()
572			if !strings.HasSuffix(name, "/") {
573				name += "/"
574			}
575			require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
576				Name:     name,
577				Mode:     0o755,
578				Typeflag: tar.TypeDir,
579			}))
580		}
581		writeFile := func(name, body string) {
582			t.Helper()
583			require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
584				Name:     name,
585				Mode:     0o644,
586				Size:     int64(len(body)),
587				Typeflag: tar.TypeReg,
588			}))
589			_, err := tw.Write([]byte(body))
590			require.NoError(t, err)
591		}
592	
593		writeDir(rootName)
594		writeFile(filepath.Join(rootName, "package.yaml"), manifest)
595		writeDir(filepath.Join(rootName, "skills"))
596		writeDir(filepath.Join(rootName, "skills", "bad-skill"))
597	
598		require.NoError(t, tw.Close())
599		require.NoError(t, gz.Close())
600		return buf.Bytes()
601	}

12	// ResolvePlugin returns the on-disk path and the resolution layer for a plugin.
13	// Precedence: project → global → baked-in.
14	//
15	// Layer values:
16	//   - "project": found under <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>
17	//   - "global":  found under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>
18	//   - "baked-in": embedded default; only valid for the "ddx" plugin name
19	//
20	// For baked-in, path is empty — callers should use defaultplugin.FS().
21	// Any other unresolved name returns a non-nil error.
22	func ResolvePlugin(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, err error) {
23		projectPluginPath := filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), "plugins", name)
24		if info, statErr := os.Stat(projectPluginPath); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
25			return projectPluginPath, "project", nil
26		}
27	
28		globalPluginPath := filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name)
29		if info, statErr := os.Stat(globalPluginPath); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
30			return globalPluginPath, "global", nil
31		}
32	
33		if name == "ddx" {
34			return "", "baked-in", nil
35		}
36	
37		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("plugin %q not found in project, global, or baked-in layers", name)

name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
        for pkg in $packages; do
          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go build - only for changed packages
    go-build:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Build"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: 'go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...'
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
    evidence-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:
        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"

    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
    secrets:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Scan for secrets"
      run: |
        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
        else
          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')

          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
            echo "Potential secrets detected"
            exit 1
          fi

          # AWS keys
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
            exit 1
          fi

          # GitHub tokens
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
            exit 1
          fi
        fi
      exclude:
        - "*.md"
        - "go.sum"
        - "package-lock.json"
        - "*_test.go"
        - "**/dist/**"
        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"

    # Binary and large file check
    large-files:
      name: "Check file sizes"

`ddx install` and `ddx update` only ever target the per-project plugin tree. There is no way to install a skill/persona/plugin once per machine, so operators must repeat installs in every project. Three install destinations must be supported: global (XDG), project in-tree (`<project>/.ddx`), and project convention (XDG projects/...).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:36 `RunE: f.runInstall` and its flag set (install.go:38-39) have no `--global` flag; install always targets the project under `.ddx/plugins/<name>/` (install.go:27-28).
- cli/cmd/update.go:57 `runUpdate` and update.go:194 `performUpdate` operate only on the working dir's project tree; update.go:112 `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir)` is project-scoped.
- Both route through ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and never reference the global layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx install` (cli/cmd/install.go) and `ddx update` (cli/cmd/update.go).
2. install --global: write content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` (via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from the resolver bead) and create symlinks/copies into agent-tier paths `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>` (copy on platforms lacking symlink support).
3. install (no --global): keep writing to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/` and link into `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` AND `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`. In in-tree mode the target is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`; in convention mode it is `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` and the project agent-tier links point into that XDG path.
4. update --global updates only the global plugin tree; update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode (in-tree vs convention) is active, via `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets`.

NON-SCOPE
- The plugin resolver/precedence (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution) — consume `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from it.
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing per-project install; both modes coexist.

The commit landed cleanly (`2b3ff49f0`) and the working tree is clean. All acceptance criteria are met:

| AC | Evidence |
|----|----------|
| 1. `install --global` → XDG global + `~/.{claude,agents}/skills/<name>` | `TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks` ✓ |
| 2. `install` (in-tree) → `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>` + project links | `TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks` ✓ |
| 3. `install` (convention) → XDG `projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` + links into it | `TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks` ✓ |
| 4. `update --global` updates only global, project untouched | `TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject` ✓ |
| 5. `update` (no flag) respects active mode | `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` ✓ |
| 6. `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/...` green | both `ok`, exit 0 ✓ |
| 7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes | exit 0, all gates ✓ |

**What changed:**
- `cli/cmd/install.go` — `--global`/`--silent` flags; new `installLayered` core (copies plugin tree into `<root>/plugins/<name>`, links `<name>` into both agent-tier skill surfaces → `<pluginDir>/skills/<name>`, symlink with copy fallback), plus `runInstallGlobal`, `installConvention`, `resolveInstallSource`, `recordInstalledEntry`.
- `cli/internal/registry/installer.go` — exported `FetchPackageSource` to materialize a release tarball into a temp source dir.
- `cli/cmd/update.go` + `command_factory_commands.go` — `--global` flag; `performUpdateGlobal` (global-only refresh) and `refreshProjectPluginTree` (mode-aware project refresh).
- Tests: added AC1–AC5; removed the obsolete `TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved` (it asserted the now-restored flag was gone — this bead reinstates `--global`, but pointed at the XDG global layer, not `$HOME/.ddx`, so it stays consistent with FEAT-015's no-`$HOME/.ddx/plugins` invariant).

The in-tree no-`--global` registry path deliberately retains the proven manifest installer to avoid regressing multi-skill plugins; the new layered installer is used for the global and convention layers.
